mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50 percent of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65 percent of instances where the unemployment rate crosses above the 6.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and consumer prices indicate demand-pull inflation that forces monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and CPI yield positive forward returns in more than 55 percent of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: Industrial production increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, with a t-statistic below 1.5 across out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day returns across all market cycles, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 500 thousand units signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of months where housing starts drop below the 500 thousand threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and industrial production confirms broad-based economic growth that boosts corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Co-movement of real GDP and industrial production fails to predict positive quarterly returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price indices above 220 trigger aggressive central bank rate hikes that discount future equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during high-inflation regimes.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL levels above 220 do not precede negative 20-day returns, with a signal Sharpe ratio below -0.5 over rolling 5-year windows.
